behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: manifest parsing/resolution plus vulnerability-check input assembly (MOCK-MISS=yes). REAL-INFRA is not required by this behavior text; it does not require real network/OS, only that extracted deps are passed correctly. Rejected System because behavior text only says dependencies are "included in the vulnerability check," not that real API/network behavior must be exercised.
behaviors.test_description: Run parser/resolver + check-orchestrator together on fixture manifests (including transitive deps) with an in-process fake vulnerability client; assert every expected direct/transitive package@version sent for checking.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This is about query construction/response-matching correctness across modules (manifest version-range handling + query/filter logic). No real LLM. No required real infra in behavior text. Rejected System because text does not require real HTTP semantics; exact-range matching can fail and be caught with contract-level fake responses.
behaviors.test_description: Wire manifest-range parser + query builder + result matcher against a fake vulnerability API response set containing same-name packages with different vulnerable ranges; assert only CVEs matching the manifest’s exact ranges are returned.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Pure ordering logic within one function/object boundary; no module boundary required, no infra, no LLM, no multi-step agentic flow.
behaviors.test_description: Call the sort function with mixed severities and CVSS ties; assert strict severity precedence and descending CVSS within each severity bucket.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure typically appears at parser/editor/serializer boundary (data round-trip and targeted patching), not just isolated comparison logic. No required real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected System because behavior text is about content correctness of modification, not filesystem-event or external-tool behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use real manifest read/parse/update/serialize modules in-process on representative files; assert only the targeted dependency version changes and all other keys/versions remain byte-equivalent or semantically identical per format rules.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly requires "real repository" PR creation. This needs real external API/network/git integration (REAL-INFRA=yes), and mocks can hide auth/branch/diff/application failures (MOCK-HIDE=yes).
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test repository, run the PR creation path end-to-end: apply one dependency bump, push branch, create PR, then verify remote PR exists and diff contains exactly one dependency version change.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver comparison is local deterministic logic in one boundary; no module orchestration required by behavior text, no infra, no LLM.
behaviors.test_description: Feed semver pairs into the breaking-change detector (e.g., 1.x->2.x, 0.x edge cases, prereleases) and assert correct breaking/non-breaking classification.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Failure is orchestration/state handoff between semver decision, PR path gating, and output/reporting modules (MOCK-MISS=yes). Rejected System: behavior does not require real repository interaction, only that PR creation is skipped and flag is surfaced.
behaviors.test_description: Run decision + orchestration + reporting modules together with a breaking bump input and a fake PR client; assert PR client is not invoked and scanner output includes the review-required flag.
